meaning: adj.不能破损的,牢不可破的
examples: The great friendship and militant solidarity between the peoples of our two countries are unbreakable.
This new material is virtually unbreakable.
Tableware for outdoor use should ideally be unbreakable.
Here, through so many difficult years, is where she displayed such unbreakable courage and determination.
Just unbreakable 60 frames per second animation of hundreds of 3D surfaces with physics.
